title: crepis bupleurifolia
intro: Crepis bupleurifolia, also known as the golden hawk's beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of China and has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion, as well as having antioxidant and anti-aging effects. Crepis bupleurifolia can be prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
